claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 8 years old, and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Now I need to ask you a few questions about your situation with Alex.

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question:

**Do you have the right to reside in the UK? For example, are you a UK citizen, do you have settled status, or do you have another immigration status that allows you to reside here?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen, so I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age limit of 16 (or under 20 if in approved education or training)
- You live in the UK and have the right to reside here as a UK citizen
- Alex lives with you, which means you're responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the eligibility requirements and can proceed with your Child Benefit claim. If you haven't already applied, you can make a claim for Child Benefit for Alex.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here as a UK citizen. Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for the child under the rules (you're normally responsible for a child if you live with them). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which satisfies the requirement that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex as he lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All the standard eligibility requirements are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here as a UK citizen. Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for the child under the rules (you're normally responsible for a child if you live with them). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which satisfies the requirement that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex as he lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All the standard eligibility requirements are satisfied.
